articles.author: Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Bernard McGhee
articles.description: U.S. forces on Monday launched an effort, announced by President Donald Trump, to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz, where hundreds have been stuck since the Iran war began.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump launches new effort to reopen Strait of Hormuz

articles.author: Aniruddha Ghosal, Jim Gomez
articles.description: For millions of foreign workers in the Mideast, the Iran war has made life even more precarious. At least 28 were killed in missile and drone attacks before a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April. Now they face an even sharper version of a long-standing dilemma: Keep working in the Gulf, where wages are much higher, hoping that the truce endures, or return to already poor countries where prices have soared because of the conflict.
articles.title: Iran war sharpens the dilemmas faced by foreign workers in the Mideast

articles.author: Elaine Kurtenbach
articles.description: Shares are mixed in Asia after U.S. stocks set more records, powered by strong profits for major companies. Oil prices held steady after U.S. President Donald Trump said the U.S. would help ships leave the Strait of Hormuz, starting on Monday. Iran has rejected the plan, but Trump also said talks with Iran could lead to positive outcomes. On Friday, the S&P 500 climbed 0.3% to another all-time high, closing out a fifth straight winning week. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dipped 0.3%, and the Nasdaq composite added 0.9% to its own record. Apple led the way after delivering better profit than expected.
articles.title: Asian markets are mixed and oil holds steady

articles.author: Cara Anna
articles.description: The ceasefire in the Iran war faces its most critical moment yet as the United States begins efforts to open the Strait of Hormuz. On Monday, the U.S. launched "Project Freedom" to guide ships through the strait, calling it a humanitarian effort. Iran views this as a violation of the ceasefire. The U.S. says two U.S.-flagged ships have safely transited, but Iran warns that any foreign military force in the strait will be targeted. Already, the United Arab Emirates says it has come under attack for the first time since the early April ceasefire, and a British military monitor says two cargo vessels are ablaze.
articles.title: What to know as US effort to open the Strait of Hormuz shakes a ceasefire

articles.author: Mark Sherman, Geoff Mulvihill
articles.description: The Supreme Court has restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortion is provided across the nation. The justices’ order Monday allows women seeking abortions to obtain the pill at pharmacies or through the mail, without an in-person visit to a doctor. Those rules had been in effect for several years until a federal appeals court imposed new restrictions last week. Most abortions are obtained with medication, normally mifepristone and a second drug, misoprostol. Their availability has made abortion accessible to women in states with bans, including Louisiana. Louisiana sued, saying mifepristone’s availability undermined the ban there.
articles.title: Supreme Court ensures telehealth access to abortion pill mifepristone

articles.author: Lindsey Tanner, Matthew Perrone
articles.description: Medication abortions became the preferred method for ending pregnancy in the U.S. even before the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Now threatened by a federal court case in Texas, they usually involve taking two prescription medicines days apart — at home or in a clinic. In the United States, medication abortions usually involve the drugs mifepristone and misoprostol. Demand for the pills is expected to grow but they have been targeted by abortion foes including Christian conservatives seeking to overturn the Food and Drug Administration’s approval of mifepristone. A hearing on the suit was held Wednesday in Texas.
articles.title: Medication abortions are under fire: Here's how they work

articles.author: Nicholas Riccardi
articles.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has escalated a nationwide redistricting war by removing one of the few remaining limits on partisan gerrymandering. The decision gutted a key provision of the Voting Rights Act that affected how states draw congressional maps, a powerful tool for influencing elections. Redistricting conflicts are exacerbating a winner-take-all style of politics that has strained American democracy in recent years. There has been extreme rhetoric, political violence and attempts to reshape elections. The rules and norms that once helped smooth over an unruly country’s vast differences have given way to a race for power at all costs.
articles.title: Redistricting war accelerates winner-take-all political combat in US

articles.description: An AP analysis shows members of Congress are announcing their exit plans at a record pace, opening up dozens of seats and putting both chambers on track for high turnover in the midterm elections.
articles.title: Tracking turnover in Congress

articles.author: Kim Chandler, Travis Loller
articles.description: Republican governors in Alabama and Tennessee have called lawmakers into special sessions seeking new congressional districts. The U.S. Supreme Court weakened a key provision of the Voting Rights Act last week. Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey has called legislators back to Montgomery starting Monday. The special session is centered on enabling a special primary election. Tennessee Republican Gov. Bill Lee has also announced a special session starting Tuesday. Republicans are eyeing the possibility of getting new lines in place for the 2026 midterm elections, or at least for 2028. Democrats are sharply criticizing the decision to try to change maps ahead of looming elections.
articles.title: Alabama and Tennessee seek new congressional districts

articles.author: Nell Clark, Mallika Sen
articles.description: Met Gala guests will interpret the dress code “Fashion is Art ” at this year’s extravaganza. Stars expected include Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams and Vogue’s Anna Wintour.
articles.title: Met Gala 2026 live updates: Stars prepare to dress for ‘Fashion is Art’ theme
